rationale: Postcondition is H ==> equal(r1,r2) where H = (r1.0==F) && (!r1.0 ==> r1.1==G) && (r2.0==F) && (!r2.0 ==> r2.1==G) with F = forall i in [lo,hi]: self_@[keys[i]]@==v@ and G = (self_@[keys[hi]]@!=v@) && (forall i in [lo,hi): ...==v@). When F is true, both r1.0 and r2.0 are true and r1.1, r2.1 are entirely unconstrained, so r1.1 != r2.1 is satisfiable (e.g. via g_r1_1_is_true && g_r2_1_is_false flags). The implication is therefore not a tautology and cannot be discharged without an axiom. Previous session-1 attempted decomposed lets and helper asserts; only the full implication assertion failed. Forbidden constructs (assume/admit/external_body) are the only way to close this, so we report give_up.
